Add /api/features endpoint for mobile feature toggles

Implements the feature toggle endpoint that the mobile app calls at startup
to determine which features to enable. Without this endpoint, all features
default to disabled (no login button, no puritan search).

Matches DO behavior: puritan-search and authentication enabled for
Android versionCode >= 6 and iOS CFBundleVersion >= 2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: change page/pageSize/id schema types from string to number

- PaginationQuerySchema: use z.coerce.number() for page and pageSize

- Remove manual Number() conversions in getTasksHandler

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

🐛 Auto-discover grammars in ~/.local/lib/tree-sitter and linuxbrew

GrammarFinder#search_paths previously only checked system-wide dirs
(/usr/lib, /usr/local/lib, /opt/homebrew/lib), missing the standard
XDG user-local install path used by the tree-sitter CLI and most
language-specific tooling.

Changes:
- Add private GrammarFinder#user_search_dirs that resolves
  ~/.local/lib/tree-sitter and ~/.local/lib via Dir.home at call
  time (so HOME expansion is always current)
- search_paths now inserts user-local paths between extra_paths and
  BASE_SEARCH_DIRS, so user installs take priority over system ones
- BASE_SEARCH_DIRS gains /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/lib for Linuxbrew
- PathValidator#trusted_directories gains the same user-local dirs
  dynamically, so find_library_path_safe also accepts them
- Add specs asserting user-local paths are present and ordered before
  system paths

With this change, gems that use tree-sitter grammars (json-merge,
bash-merge, etc.) auto-discover grammars without needing explicit
TREE_SITTER_*_PATH env vars in mise.toml on a fresh checkout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tree_haver
repository.description: 🌴 TreeHaver is a cross-Ruby adapter for many AST parsing libraries; supporting MRI Ruby, JRuby, & TruffleRuby. Provides unified parsing API & AST when using ruby_tree_sitter, parslet, citrus, ffi, tree_stump (Rust), JRuby JARs, etc. As Faraday is to HTTP clients, this is for ASTs: "Learn once & write once & run anywhere"

fix(repo): step through npm@10 before upgrading to npm@latest in deploy workflow (#56)

Directly installing `npm@latest` (npm 11) from older npm versions
triggers a known upgrade bug. The deploy workflow must first land on npm
10 before jumping to 11.

## Changes

- **`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`**: Split the single `npm install -g
npm@latest` step into two sequential installs:
  ```yaml
  run: |
    npm install -g npm@10
    npm install -g npm@latest
  ```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: AgentEnder <6933928+AgentEnder@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Import full Puritan Library (7117 works, 1053 authors)

- Rewrote import script to use Worker /admin/import endpoint with D1 bound
  params, bypassing the 100KB SQL statement size limit
- Store 2KB content preview per work in D1 (full content via R2 when enabled)
- Replaced FTS5 fulltext search with LIKE-based search (FTS5 index was
  consuming too much of D1's 500MB free tier limit)
- DB size: 29.9MB with all 7117 works and 1053 authors
- Removed temporary /admin/import endpoint after successful import

commit.message:
Fix GPT-5.4 + Goldeneye review findings (6 issues)

1. Missing icons: Add LayoutList, Wrench, Coins, FileEdit, Square to Icon.jsx
2. History not sent: Use messagesRef to read history synchronously (was
   relying on React state updater side effect, which runs asynchronously)
3. Bold turn refs not clickable: Remove bold wrapping from longest answer
   so [Turn N] renders as a clickable ref button
4. Session leak on error: Always disconnect() before nulling _persistentSession
5. Session context bleeding: Add /api/qa/reset endpoint, called by clear()
   to invalidate server-side persistent session
6. Regex false positives: Move 'longest' pattern first, tighten which/tools
   and which/files to require verb (were/was/did), prevent 'which tool took
   the most time?' from matching tools instead of falling through to model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4: Rich markdown rendering, clickable turn refs, new tests

QADrawer.jsx:
- Enhanced parseMessageContent with support for code blocks, inline code,
  headers (h1-h3), list items (bullet points), and newlines
- New renderParts function handles all part types with themed styles
- parseInline uses a single combined regex for turn refs + bold + code

qaClassifier.js:
- Turn detail answers now use plain [Turn N] format (not bold-wrapped)
  so the parser correctly produces clickable ref buttons

qaDrawer.test.jsx:
- Added tests: inline code rendering, Files/Longest insights visibility,
  turn ref clickable buttons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3: Persistent SDK session, conversation history, batched tokens

qaAgent.js:
- Persistent SDK session across Q&A calls (no more create/disconnect per question)
- Session auto-invalidated on error, recreated on next call
- Conversation history injected into prompt for follow-up context
- Guard against duplicate content.delta + assistant.message output
- Stream status events (onStatus callback) for richer UI feedback

useQA.js:
- Send conversation history (last 10 messages) to server
- 50ms token batching to reduce React re-renders during streaming
- Handle server-side status events and update streamingStatus

routes/ai.js:
- Stream initial 'Analyzing session...' status event before model call
- Forward onStatus events from qaAgent to SSE stream

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

KETTLE_DEV_DEBUG=true is the correct variable to disable output
silencing in specs and enable kettle-rb debug logging.

repository.name: tree_haver
repository.description: 🌴 TreeHaver is a cross-Ruby adapter for many AST parsing libraries; supporting MRI Ruby, JRuby, & TruffleRuby. Provides unified parsing API & AST when using ruby_tree_sitter, parslet, citrus, ffi, tree_stump (Rust), JRuby JARs, etc. As Faraday is to HTTP clients, this is for ASTs: "Learn once & write once & run anywhere"

commit.message:
Redesign Q&A drawer: Quick insights, stop button, lifted state

Phase 1 of Q&A improvement plan:

- Replace SuggestedChips with Quick insights panel (Summary, Tools,
  Errors, Cost buttons with icon labels instead of fake chat questions)
- Add Stop button wired to qa.abort() during streaming
- Lift useQA hook from QADrawer to AppSessionView so conversation
  state persists across drawer open/close
- Add multi-phase streaming status (Analyzing session / Generating answer)
- Add unique message IDs instead of array index keys
- Show 'quick answer' vs 'AI answer' badge on response bubbles
- Fix frontend timeout to cancel the reader on timeout (was fire-and-forget)
- Align frontend timeout to 60s to match backend SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS
- Fix abort() to preserve partial streamed content
- Reuse classifier context instead of rebuilding it
- Fix getUserMessages() to return last N messages not first N
- Update QADrawer tests for new prop interface and Quick insights UI
- Add test for quick answer badge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

style(ui): redesign calendar view with month header rows

Month names now render as full-width header rows (uppercase, with
monthly total on the right) with individual items indented below,
matching the group/sub-category pattern used on the Budget page.

commit.message:
Add pr-review and sync-artifacts skills

Two new Copilot skills in .github/skills/:

- pr-review: Opinionated autonomous PR reviewer with 7 passes
  (UI/UX compliance, duplicate code, dead code, missing tests,
  architecture drift, four-artifact sync, slop detection)

- sync-artifacts: Four-artifact sync enforcer -- detects drift
  in README, CLAUDE.md, style guide, and screenshots after UI
  changes, then fixes them. Screenshots use Playwright MCP
  browser tools with demo data only (no personal logs).

commit.message:
Sea routes require 95%+ water tiles in all code paths

Fixed 4 locations where sea routes were created:
- generateSeaRoutes() primary loop: already had 95% check (no change)
- generateSeaRoutes() fallback loop: was skipping water check entirely
- Town founding (runtime): was using 30% threshold via checkWaterPath()
- buildNewSeaRoute(): had no water validation at all
- Post-load orphan port fixup: was creating routes with no water check

All now use findTerrainPath() with waterFraction >= 0.95 threshold.
Routes that fail validation are simply not created — ports without
enough open water between them won't get fake sea routes over land.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
